name: LADWP Residential R-1(A) Standard Service — Zone 1
utility: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (municipal)

provenance.notes: RECONSTRUCTED for the Jul-Sep 2025 factor quarter, Zone 1 High Season (Jun-Sep); tiers are monthly-kWh (LADWP bills bi-monthly at 2x thresholds). Total rate per tier = base energy charge + adjustment factors. Base (filed vintage, unchanged since the 2016 incremental ordinance's final step; confirmed identical in USURDB entries eff 2022-09-01 and 2025-01-01, both sourced to LADWP's official rate summary): Tier1 $0.07142, Tier2 $0.13001, Tier3-summer $0.21702 (from USURDB 631ba5d4757e95617e6d6128; the 2025 USURDB entry miscurates summer T3 as = T2). Factors: composite ECAF $0.11212/kWh eff 2025-07-01 (FILED - LADWP board letter 'Energy Cost Adjustment Expenditures... July 1, 2025', ladwp.com/sites/default/files/2025-07/, read directly) + other factors (ESA+RCA+IRCA) $0.04655 = USURDB Q1-2025 adj snapshot $0.15154 minus filed Jan-2025 ECAF $0.10499 (board letter, read directly). Cross-check: same method with Apr-Jun ECAF $0.10968 reproduces solar.com's published June-2025 totals 22.8/28.6/37.3 c/kWh to the rounding digit (solar.com/learn/understanding-ladwp-electric-rates/). fixed_monthly_usd $7.90 = Power Access Charge for a Tier-2 customer (PAC is usage-tiered: $2.30/$7.90/$22.70 by highest tier reached; $10/mo minimum charge exists; both published + USURDB). Export: LADWP retail-rate net metering, credits roll over, muni exempt from CPUC NEM-3.0 (published: energysage.com/local-data/net-metering/ladwp/, dsireusa.org program 4855).
